qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Standalone live recording helper exposes recorded stream files without authentication
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding is an unauthenticated file-disclosure path in plugin/Live/standAloneFiles/getRecordedFile.php, while the advisory is a SQL injection in objects/like.php::getLike() caused by concatenating videos_id into a partially parameterized query. The file, sink, and root cause do not match.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described SQL injection in objects/like.php::getLike() or an equivalent root cause.
evidence: The advisory describes SQL injection in objects/like.php where getLike() concatenates attacker-controlled videos_id into a SELECT query while only users_id remains parameterized.
The only final qualifying finding in the session targets plugin/Live/standAloneFiles/getRecordedFile.php and describes unauthenticated streaming of /var/www/tmp/<recording>.flv files.
That finding does not involve the likes query, getLike(), videos_id concatenation, or any SQL sink, so it is a separate open-world issue in the same repository.
